bead.title: fix: isolate MCP contract tests under parallel execution
bead.description:
Context: Full `cargo test` currently fails in `crates/axon-server/tests/mcp_contract.rs` under default parallel execution with `no such table: databases` in `mcp_axon_query_matches_graphql_policy_semantics` and `mcp_nexiq_reference_policy_queries_match_graphql`. The same contract test file passes with `--test-threads=1`, so this is a test isolation/shared-state leak.

In-scope files: `crates/axon-server/tests/mcp_contract.rs` and shared test fixture helpers it directly uses.
Out-of-scope: weakening MCP/GraphQL parity assertions, serializing the whole workspace globally, or skipping tests.
Implementation hint: isolate database names/temp dirs/server state per test, remove shared mutable test resources, or make fixture setup idempotent under parallel execution.
bead.acceptance:
1. `cargo test -p axon-server --test mcp_contract` passes with default test parallelism.
2. `cargo test -p axon-server --test mcp_contract -- --test-threads=1` passes.
3. `cargo test` passes far enough to no longer fail with `no such table: databases` from MCP contract tests.
